text:
This paper deals with the influence of the international integration of education – primarily the effects of the Bologna and Copenhagen processes – on national professional education systems. The authors characterise the international integration of education in several dimensions, such as regulation, educational strategy, structural solutions, quality assurance and curriculum and training design modernisation.

The study focuses on the competitive position of the Russian market for educational services and the core competitive advantages and disadvantages of this market. The research explores the pedagogical, social and economic factors and consequences of bringing professional education in line with global standards, as well as the socio-economic consequences for society of enrolment of foreign students in Russian universities. The study's results are intended to provide assistance for the international cooperation departments of vocational schools wishing to bring Russian VET within the global framework of the exchange of educational and academic services. The study's results may also be applied to the development of staff training programmes for university administrators who will have to navigate the new global context of higher education.
